sections.biography-source-undefined: Xun Yi was the second son of Xun Yun. Because he was the maternal grandson of Cao Cao, he was treated with favor by the Wei emperors. By 249, he held the titles of General of the Interior Camp, District Marquis of Wuchang. That year, he supported the deposition of Cao Fang.

full_text:
Xun Yi 荀霬
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Xun Yi (荀霬)was born in Yingyin county, Yingchuan commandery. An in-law of the Simas. Xun Yi was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Xun Yi was the second son of Xun Yun. Because he was the maternal grandson of Cao Cao, he was treated with favor by the Wei emperors. By 249, he held the titles of General of the Interior Camp, District Marquis of Wuchang. That year, he supported the deposition of Cao Fang.
